title: Flavorful Smoky Slovakian Pork Dumplings
description: Smoky and pillowy Slovakian pork dumplings wrapped in pandan leaves, perfect for a solo dinner, featuring the warmth of bay leaf and a rest period for optimal flavor, this dish is also gluten-free-friendly.

ingredients.name: ground pork
ingredients.notes: preferably with some fat for flavor

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, smoked paprika, bay leaves, onion, garlic, salt, and black pepper, mixing well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
Remove the mixture from the refrigerator and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Lay a pandan leaf flat on a work surface, with the stem end facing you.
Place a small amount of the pork mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package, repeating with the remaining leaves and filling.
Place the dumplings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each dumpling.
Bake the dumplings for 25 minutes, or until they are cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 160 degrees Fahrenheit.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with additional bay leaves if desired.
